payload.text:
Build a premium, editorial-grade website for Kromah Lab, a professional education and consulting business that develops science-driven hair coloring systems for Asian hair and other dark, resistant hair types. Kromah Lab solves a critical industry problem: Western hair color education and product systems are not engineered for high-melanin, resistant fibers, resulting in unpredictable lift, unnecessary damage, and inconsistent long-term results. Through proprietary methodologies—most notably the Liquid Hair Methodology—Kromah Lab replaces guesswork with repeatable, chemistry-based systems focused on controlled lightening, precise pigment refinement, and preservation of fiber integrity. The business serves elite hairstylists, educators, hair brands, manufacturers, and R&D teams through advanced education, system design, and strategic consulting, positioning Kromah Lab as a global authority at the intersection of hair science, cosmetic chemistry, and professional color innovation. Use the visual and structural aesthetic of https://clinical-luxury-22.aura.build/
 as inspiration, emphasizing a clean, clinical-luxury design with minimalist layouts, strong typography, neutral tones, refined spacing, and an authoritative, laboratory-meets-editorial feel.

**Defining the Website Vision**

I am now focused on defining the core elements of this website project. My thought process revolves around establishing the specific identity of the React website for Kromah Lab. This involves analyzing the desired "clinical-luxury" aesthetic and mapping it to the core values: science-driven authority, minimalism, high-end editorial design, and a laboratory feel. This is the bedrock of the project.


**Refining the Target Audience**

I'm now zeroing in on the target audience: elite stylists, educators, and R&D teams. This clarifies the need for Liquid Hair Methodology, system design, strategic consulting, and advanced education as key offerings. I'm visualizing the "clinical-luxury" aesthetic with its off-whites, charcoals, and potential silver accents, guided by clean sans-serif and technical typography, and the "lab coat chic" vibe is growing clearer.


**Structuring the UI Architecture**

I'm now architecting the UI, deciding on a Single Page Application (SPA) structure. I plan to use section references for a long-scroll landing page with distinct sections, avoiding complex routing. This approach will offer a full site feel. I'm focusing on the main layout (`App.tsx`), along with key components such as a sticky, minimal `Header.tsx`, a high-impact, editorial-style `Hero.tsx`, and sections dedicated to the Liquid Hair methodology and services (`Methodology.tsx`, `Services.tsx`).


**Developing the Component Library**

I'm now building out the component library. I am planning the structure of the various website components to ensure a cohesive look and feel. I am focusing on the `Footer.tsx` which will be minimal. I'm building out a general `Section.tsx` component to handle consistent padding and layout. I'll use `lucide-react` for the website icons.



**Outlining the Core Structure**

I'm now drafting the component hierarchy, concentrating on core files like `App.tsx`, `index.html`, and `index.tsx`. The `App` component will manage the active section for smooth scrolling. I am considering a fixed `Header` with a blur effect. The `Hero` section will have editorial layout. I'm focusing on high contrast text in the About/Problem section, and technical diagram feel for the Methodology section. I'll use a grid for the services with minimal cards and a simple footer.


**Integrating AI Assistant**

I'm now integrating an AI feature subtly to showcase expertise, opting for a "Kromah AI Scientist" assistant. This will be a drawer or modal, accessible from the site. I'll focus on answering questions about the methodology using Gemini, such as questions like "Why is lifting Asian hair difficult?". I am planning to use `gemini-3-flash-preview` for this purpose to align with the R&D theme.

entries.description: Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS CDN and font configuration.
High-impact hero section with editorial layout.
